Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you lose all prior milestone feature benefits from this thread and gain the following benefits.

Burden’s Shield [1–5, Saving Throws, Ability Checks]. Your AC is reduced by 2. When this occurs, the AC of another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you increases by 2. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Burden’s Shield is triggered, you can choose to reduce your AC by 4 instead of 2. If you do so, the AC of the chosen creature increases by 4 (instead of 2), or you can choose for it to receive the benefit of the Tide of Fate’s Greater Sacrifice. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Tides of Fate [1–3, Saving Throws]. Your Speed is reduced by 10 feet. When this occurs, the Speed of another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you increases by 10 feet. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Tides of Fate is triggered, you can choose to reduce your Speed to 0 until the start of your next turn. If you do so, in addition to the normal benefit, the chosen creature does not provoke opportunity attacks and ignores Difficult Terrain until the end of your next turn. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Explorer (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

Glory lies just over the horizon. Time to find it.

Explorers occupy an unusual place in Northlands society. Though revered for their courage, they rarely find a comfortable place in a community. Driven by wanderlust, curiosity, boredom, or a simple inability to relate to civilized folks, the Explorer spends most of their time in the wilderness. Some climb mountains, others set sail for lost lands, and still others delve through dark tunnels beneath the earth. For an Explorer, the greatest treasure awaits beyond the next hill.

Whether by choice or by fate, you follow the path less traveled. Perhaps you’re a rugged seafarer or mountaineer seeking discoveries in the wild. Other Explorers are treasure hunters, royal mapmakers, or seekers of truth. You might be an exile, outlaw, or iconoclast, who simply can’t get along with others. Or you might be driven by vision or prophecy to forsake hearth and home for the unknown.

 Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Explorer Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Rangers in the party. Consult with your Ranger players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Explorer Goals
d6 Goal
1 I shall find a route through the Bloodfjord Mountains never before traveled by mortals.
2 To avert Ragnarok, I must carry a prophecy to a far-flung land.
3 My mother died searching for the headwaters of the Tearstain River. I shall complete her quest.
4 I seek a sapling of the World Tree, rumored to be growing on a tiny island in the Nieder Strait.
5 Once I decipher the ancient map I hold, I’ll delve into the depths of Narvegr Maw.
6 The Frozen Palace of the last Hyperborean lies somewhere in the Bleak Expanse. I’ll find it and return with his icy crown.

options.label: Explorer (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Traversal Expert. You have a knack for finding easy paths through the environment. You may use your Bonus Action to move your Speed. During this movement, you ignore Difficult Terrain and have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to climb, jump, or swim. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Scout’s Awareness. You have a preternatural sense that allows you to avoid dangers in your surroundings. For 1 hour, you and each ally within 30 feet who can see you have Advantage on saving throws to resist the effects of nonmagical traps and hazards. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

options.label: Explorer (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Ennobled. A monarch has recognized your achievements as an Explorer in their or other territories by granting you a noble title, which comes with a moderately sized estate. You have Advantage on Charisma checks with anyone who would be impressed or intimidated by your title. You also can avoid punishment for minor infractions, though if you abuse this, your title might be revoked.

Wayfarer’s Steps. You can never become lost by nonmagical means and can always retrace your steps. Additionally, as a Bonus Action, you can take the Dash action and command up to 4 willing creatures who can hear you to use their Reaction to immediately take a Dash action. Neither you nor your allies trigger Opportunity Attacks while moving in this way. Once you use this Bonus Action, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Far Traveler. While traveling, you and your companions may travel at a Fast Pace without any penalties. You always find good campsites and have Advantage on any d20 rolls related to camping or foraging in the wilderness. You also have Advantage on Animal Handling checks to calm wild animals.

One With the Land. You may study the land from a good vantage point (such as a high position or advantageous overlook) for 1 minute and ask a question concerning the natural world or locations within 5 miles. This functions like the Commune with Nature spell. Once you use this feature, you must wait 24 hours before using it again.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

Your fate is written. Make it glorious.

Many warriors loudly boast of their desire for a noble death or an honorable end in battle, to thus earn a seat in the great hall of Valhalla. These boasts are but hopeful wishes since most individuals never see the path of their future. You, however, are not most individuals. You have been blessed, or perhaps cursed, with the knowledge of your end, though this knowledge doesn’t tell you if you succeed or fail in the circumstance surrounding your death. You might know you will die facing a dark warlord, but you don’t know if you will defeat her. Despite this, the Norns have given you a rare opportunity. The knowledge you possess will help you ensure your death is worthy of the sagas.

Some of those who are Fatebound are marked from birth. Others learn of their fate from seers, shamans, or divine visions. Some heroes earn this thread after engaging in activities that move the gods or the Norns to reward them with such a potent end.

Fatebound and Death. Keep in mind that having this thread may mean you can’t die before your fated time, but it doesn’t mean you will reach that point whole and healthy. While your fate is inevitable, luck alone determines your condition when you meet that end. Do you lead a glorious charge across the battlefield? Or are you left guarding the baggage? Has every confrontation leading up to your end prepared you to defeat your ultimate foe? Or has a lifetime of avoidance left you ill-prepared?

If a Fatebound PC is killed before their time, and magical resurrection is not an option, a GM could instead choose for them to be injured in a significant way—such as the loss of a limb or eye. These injuries should not be able to be healed without high-level magic. Alternatively, perhaps they lose one of their powerful magical items instead of their lives, or they might suffer a level of Exhaustion until they reach their next milestone. The GM and player should agree on this complication.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Fatebound Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note: The Fatebound is an unusual thread in that its final milestone specifically describes the manner in which a character might meet with a permanent death, likely as part of the final accomplishment of their goal, and then empowers the PCs’ allies as a reward. Most importantly, this thread should be something the player consciously selects.

Fatebound Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will die only after finding and facing the mightiest dragon in the land.
2 I will be slain by Utgard-Loki while attempting to thwart his plan to slay the gods.
3 To defend my people from invasion, I pledge to pay any price, including my own life.
4 I shall search for and find a fabled lost treasure, but I will die in the process.
5 I will rise to become the North’s greatest reaver, then perish battling the World Serpent.
6 I seek the truth behind the fall of Nordheim, though it will cost my life to learn it.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Strength of Wyrd. While you are Bloodied, once per turn you may add your Proficiency Bonus to a damage roll. You may use this damage bonus a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Enduring Wyrd. When you take damage, you may use your Reaction to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You cannot use this feature again until you complete a Short or Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Doom Delayed. When you would be killed, you may instead declare yourself as Stable with 0 Hit Points. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Knight of Fate. You have become a legend in a territory, and the monarch there has knighted you, granting you holdings that will revert back to the monarchy upon your death. You have Advantage on Charisma-based checks with the people of this land and are frequently showered with minor gifts and favors there.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Strength of Wyrd. While you are Bloodied, once per turn you may add your Proficiency Bonus to a damage roll. You may use this damage bonus a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Last Act of Fate. At a moment you have predetermined to be the time when you are to die, when you are reduced 0 Hit Points or killed outright, you may (no action required) instead set your Hit Points to 1. You lose any detrimental conditions, and any harmful effects on you are ended immediately. Until the end of your next turn, you gain Advantage on all attack rolls and ability checks, you have Immunity to all damage and conditions (and other effects at the GM’s discretion), and you add your character level to all damage rolls.

At the end of your next turn, you die and can never be restored to life.

Glorious End. If you achieve your destiny in a way that earns you a seat in Valhalla (or some other appropriate afterlife), the allies who witness your death have Advantage on all d20 rolls for 24 hours as they are inspired by your heroism.

options.label: Herald (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Storyteller’s Knack. You learn three languages of your choice.

Performer’s Reward. If you have at least 3 days of downtime between adventures, you can earn GP equal to 10 times your character level by performing your stories in taverns, on the street, or for powerful patrons. Additional downtime events don’t increase this reward.

Your stories don’t just recount events . . . they shape history.

Storytellers are revered in the Northlands. Whether a skald sharing tales around a fire, a lore keeper gathering sagas to pass down the generations, or a grizzled reaver frightening young warriors by recalling harrowing battles, the tradition of collecting stories is respected across nearly all cultures in the region. As a Herald, you are a proud, if temporary, part of this tradition, driven by a need to spread stories during your travels to accomplish a specific end.

Perhaps your stories encourage downtrodden karls to rise up against their cruel jarl, warn a region about an approaching invasion, or convince warriors to join your cause. Part skald, part herald, part envoy, you will use your tales and stories to sway the minds of your audience and help shape the world.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Herald Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Bards in the party. Consult with your Bard players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Herald Goals
d6 Goal
1 The feared invasion from Jotunheim approaches, and the country must be warned.
2 By telling the sagas, I will gather support for the greatest raid the North has ever launched.
3 My tales flatter Thor and Wotan in the hope they will support our struggle against the forces of Chernobog and Angrboda.
4 I spread the word of the great crusade to eradicate the darkness beneath Tanserhall. Heed my call!
5 I seek a lost treasure of Hyperborea, and by spreading tales of similar treasures, I hope to find a path to a hidden vault.
6 To rouse the people against their evil king, I shall assemble an array of sorrowful tales of corruption that cannot be ignored.

options.label: Herald (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Enthralling Speaker. You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Expertise. Additionally, while performing before an audience, you can cast either Charm Person or Suggestion as part of your performance without requiring somatic or material components. Use Charisma as your spellcasting ability score. After casting one of these spells using this feature, you can’t use the feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Storyteller’s Knack. You learn three languages of your choice.

options.label: Herald (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Persuasive Words. If you make a Charisma check to change a creature's attitude or convince them of your cause, you may treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier per Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

options.label: Herald (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Heroic Saga. Your tales grow so powerful that they can briefly tamper with luck. After you spend at least 10 minutes telling an inspiring story or legend, choose either one creature who can hear you, or up to three creatures. You can’t select yourself as a target. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until for 7 days.

Depending on the number of targets, creatures can receive one of the following.

Luck Boon (Three Targets). Once before the next Long Rest, a target can expend their boon to gain Advantage on a saving throw, attack roll, or ability check.

Wyrd Boon (One Target). Once before their next Long Rest, if the target does not succeed on a saving throw or attack roll, they can choose to succeed instead. An attack roll succeeding in this way results in the minimum needed to succeed (not a Critical Hit, for example).

Song of Matrimony. A member of the royal family in a particular land has fallen in love with you and your performances, and the feeling is mutual. You are engaged to marry into the royal family.

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

Neither storm nor night nor bloody fang shall slow your relentless pursuit.

Hunting is as old as the Northlands itself. From volcanic peaks to frigid fens and aurora-bathed glaciers, the tales of hunters are sung alongside those of heroes and gods. While some may hunt for survival or vengeance, you are different. You are a hunter to be feared, as you hunt for the thrill of the chase and the glory of victory.

As a Legend Hunter, you may have been chosen by the Norns, blessed by a god, or forged in trials of blood and grift. No matter how you come to it, your extraordinary search for greater and greater prey sets you apart from those who hunt merely for survival. You do not merely forge trails; you craft sagas. You do not merely slay beasts; you end legends to become a legend.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Legend Hunter Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Legend Hunter Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will prove my valor by tracking down and slaying a legendary monster that has been destroying villages.
2 I hunt for a mythical two-headed jotun somewhere in the Bleak Expanse.
3 Besting a great dragon in its lair shall be my ultimate victory.
4 I will go beneath the waves to bring low the mother of all krakens.
5 I will bring back the head of Fenris.
6 My legend will be assured when I go among the roots of Yggdrasil to slay Nidhogg.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Unclouded Sight. Your honed senses let you ignore obscurement caused by weather, such as rain, snow, or fog. This includes magical clouds and weather effects, such as Fog Cloud and Sleet Storm, but not Darkness.

Reliable Senses. After failing an Intelligence (Investigation), Wisdom (Perception), or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can reroll the check and take the higher result. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Legend Breaker. When you hit a creature that has Legendary Resistance or Legendary Actions, you can choose to forgo dealing damage to force that creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength or Wisdom modifier and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target becomes unable use its Legendary Resistance or its Legendary Actions. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

The Ultimate Favor. You performed a service for a powerful healer, who was caught in an animal trap set by another hunter. In return, they have offered to cast a single spell of healing of 6th to 9th level on you or a creature of your choice, at a time of your choosing, even if this puts them at great risk. This should be a spell that heals, regenerates, resurrects, or that removes conditions or curses.

Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Unclouded Sight. Your honed senses let you ignore obscurement caused by weather, such as rain, snow, or fog. This includes magical clouds and weather effects, such as Fog Cloud and Sleet Storm, but not Darkness.

Reliable Senses. After failing an Intelligence (Investigation), Wisdom (Perception), or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can reroll the check and take the higher result. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Jarl’s Authority. The authority granted you by your loyal service can sway the opinions of others. Before rolling an Intimidation or Persuasion check to influence someone who knows and respects (or fears) your ruler, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as if you’d rolled a natural 15. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

You carry out the will of your king, no matter the risk.

You have pledged your loyalty to an important personage, promising to guard them or travel across the Northlands in their service, risking danger as you seek to accomplish a great task. In the past, it was expected that a huskarl would swear to serve a lord for life or until they were dismissed. While jarls still take huskarls in that way, they also accept the service of huskarls on a temporary basis, to perform specific deeds or until certain threats are dealt with. These are the types of huskarls described in this thread.

The sworn huskarl enjoys a position of respect within their lord’s retinue. More than a mere bodyguard, you are a trusted agent acting on your ruler’s behalf. You carry some of their authority with you, but that also comes with the responsibility to act in ways they would approve. Sworn huskarls who bring shame to their jarl don’t remain in service for long.

A jarl takes on a new huskarl with a simple ceremony in which the huskarl swears an oath in the longhouse before a gathering of other members of the felag. During the oath-swearing, the goals or timeframe for the service are mentioned (unless the jarl keeps your mission a secret). Traditionally, your reasons for serving are respect for the lord, payment, or power. However, in some cases your oath may hold a secret. Perhaps you made it under threat of violence or under false pretenses. Or maybe your lord doesn’t deserve such loyalty. You might serve under protest or with your own goal that’s unknown to your ruler.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Sworn Huskarl Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Sworn Huskarl Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will find my king’s long-lost child so that my kingdom will have a true heir.
2 My queen depends on me to find and slay the pretender who claims a blood right to her throne.
3 I must root out a hidden sect of the Cult of Ragnarok that grows within my jarl’s land.
4 Giant raiding parties threaten the land. My jarl calls upon me to destroy them and make the land safe once more.
5 I can only earn my freedom by finding the stolen crown of my sadistic monarch.
6 I serve only until I can discover a way to overthrow this corrupt king, who took the throne through dark sorcery.

options.description:
Patron Power: While within 100 feet of Njord with an active laukaz rune, you can call upon Njord’s power to protect yourself. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you targets you with a weapon attack, you can use your Reaction to transform your body briefly into water, causing the attack to go right through you and miss you. Then your rune ends. You can sketch and use laukaz this way as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Njord.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch laukaz in the air (no action required). When you do so, you can’t become lost while exposed to open air and can predict natural weather for a number of hours equal to your level. Additionally, if your laukaz rune is active, you can end your rune early as a Bonus Action or Reaction to gain a magical Swim Speed equal to your Speed and the ability to breathe underwater, both for 1 minute.

Lesser Power: With your laukaz rune active, you can end your rune early to surround yourself with a whirling vortex of wind for 1 minute. For the duration, Ranged weapon attacks against you have Disadvantage, and the wind hedges out any vapor, gas, and fog that a strong wind could disperse.

Greater Power: With your laukaz rune active, you can end your rune early to cause a wave of wind and water to spring into existence. The wave emanates from you in a 60- foot Cone. All creatures in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, a creature takes 2d8 Bludgeoning damage and 3d8 Cold damage, and has the Prone condition; on a success, the creature takes half as much damage and doesn’t have the Prone condition.

options.label: Perto (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch perto in the air (no action required). Upon doing do, you gain a d6, called a perto die, that you can roll, adding the result to a single d20 roll you make before the end of your next turn.

options.label: Perto (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes a d20 roll, you can use a Reaction to sketch perto. If you do so, roll your perto die. The creature’s roll increases or decreases (your choice) by the result of your roll.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch perto in the air (no action required). Upon doing do, you gain a d6, called a perto die, that you can roll, adding the result to a single d20 roll you make before the end of your next turn.

options.label: Perto (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: Your perto die increases to a d8. Additionally, after you sketch perto and add the result of your d8 roll to a d20 roll, you can hide immediately (no action required), providing the terrain and circumstances permit it.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch perto in the air (no action required). Upon doing do, you gain a d6, called a perto die, that you can roll, adding the result to a single d20 roll you make before the end of your next turn.

Lesser Power: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes a d20 roll, you can use a Reaction to sketch perto. If you do so, roll your perto die. The creature’s roll increases or decreases (your choice) by the result of your roll.

options.label: Perto (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While within 100 feet of Loki, when you sketch perto, you gain two rolls of your perto die. You must use one for yourself and apply the other to a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. You must use both rolls before the start of your next turn or they are wasted. You can sketch perto as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Loki.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch perto in the air (no action required). Upon doing do, you gain a d6, called a perto die, that you can roll, adding the result to a single d20 roll you make before the end of your next turn.

Greater Power: Your perto die increases to a d8. Additionally, after you sketch perto and add the result of your d8 roll to a d20 roll, you can hide immediately (no action required), providing the terrain and circumstances permit it.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: When you sketch sowilo, you can use a Bonus Action to inspire a Friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The creature can use its Reaction immediately to move up to half its Speed and make one melee attack.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While within 100 feet of Baldur, when you sketch sowilo, you can use an action to focus your radiance into a beam of pure light. Make a ranged spell attack against a target using your Dexterity or Charisma modifier. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 Radiant damage and has the Blinded condition until the start of your next turn. This immediately ends your rune and its aura of light. You can sketch sowilo and use it this way as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Baldur.

Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

Greater Power: When you sketch sowilo, you can use a Bonus Action to inspire a Friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The creature can use its Reaction immediately to move up to half its Speed and make one melee attack.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch thurisaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, your melee weapon attacks deal 1d4 Lightning damage until the end of your next turn. Additionally, if you deal this extra Lightning damage to a target, you can cause a clap of thunder to push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d6. In addition, if you strike a creature with a melee attack and deal the extra Lightning damage, a second creature you can see within 5 feet of your initial target also takes the extra Lightning damage.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch thurisaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, your melee weapon attacks deal 1d4 Lightning damage until the end of your next turn. Additionally, if you deal this extra Lightning damage to a target, you can cause a clap of thunder to push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d8. In addition, any melee weapon you wield gains the Thrown property with a range of 20/60 ft. If you strike a creature with a Thrown weapon, it magically reappears in your hand at the end of your turn.

Lesser Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d6. In addition, if you strike a creature with a melee attack and deal the extra Lightning damage, a second creature you can see within 5 feet of your initial target also takes the extra Lightning damage.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While within 100 feet of Thor, when you sketch thurisaz, you can use an action to strike the ground with a weapon, causing lightning to arc from the impact to strike up to three targets you can see within 60 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack using your Strength or Dexterity modifier. On a hit, a target takes 3d12 Lightning damage. Then the rune ends. You can sketch and use thurisaz this way as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Thor.

Lesser Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d6. In addition, if you strike a creature with a melee attack and deal the extra Lightning damage, a second creature you can see within 5 feet of your initial target also takes the extra Lightning damage.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch tiwaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you to become your focused target. For 1 minute, you automatically know when your focused target is lying, your focused target can’t be invisible to you, and you can pinpoint its location while it remains within 60 feet of you.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When you sketch tiwaz, if you hit your focused target with an attack, you can choose for the next attack made against it before the end of your next turn to have Advantage. If this subsequent attack hits, it deals extra damage equal to your level; then your rune ends.

For 1 minute, you automatically know when your focused target is lying, your focused target can’t be invisible to you, and you can pinpoint its location while it remains within 60 feet of you.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: When you sketch tiwaz, you can’t be Frightened by your focused target. Additionally, the first time your focused target hits you with an attack, you have Resistance against the damage type caused by that attack until the end of your next turn. Then your rune ends.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While you remain within 100 feet of Tyr, if your focused target is within 60 feet of you and deals damage to anyone other than you, you can use a Reaction to take half the damage upon yourself. If you attack the focused target on your next turn and successfully hit, you deal bonus Force damage equal to the amount of damage you took from your Reaction. Then your rune ends. You can sketch and use tiwaz this way as often as you want, provided you stay within 100 feet of Tyr.

options.label: Drow Lineage
options.description: Drow typically dwell in the Underdark and have been shaped by it. Some drow individuals and societies avoid the Underdark altogether yet carry its magic. In the Eberron setting, for example, drow dwell in rainforests and cyclopean ruins on the continent of Xen’drik.

options.label: High Elf Lineage
options.description: High elves have been infused with the magic of crossings between the Feywild and the Material Plane. On some worlds, high elves refer to themselves by other names. For example, they call themselves sun or moon elves in the Forgotten Realms setting, Silvanesti and Qualinesti in the Dragonlance setting, and Aereni in the Eberron setting.

options.label: Wood Elf Lineage
options.description: Wood elves carry the magic of primeval forests within themselves. They are known by many other names, including wild elves, green elves, and forest elves. Grugach are reclusive wood elves of the Greyhawk setting, while the Kagonesti and the Tairnadal are wood elves of the Dragonlance and Eberron settings, respectively.

class.name: Channel Divinity
class.description:
You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Divine Spark and Turn Undead, each of which is described below. Each time you use this class’s Channel Divinity, choose which Channel Divinity effect from this class to create. You gain additional effect options at higher Cleric levels.

You can use this class’s Channel Divinity twice. You regain one of its expended uses when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You gain additional uses when you reach certain Cleric levels, as shown in the Channel Divinity column of the Cleric Features table.

If a Channel Divinity effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals the spell save DC from this class’s Spellcasting feature.
class.snippet: You can channel energy directly from the Outer Planes to fuel magical effects. When you use this class’s Channel Divinity, you can choose which effect to create. You can use this class’s Channel Divinity {{scalevalue}} times per Long Rest, but can regain one expended use after finishing a Short Rest.

class.name: Channel Divinity: Divine Spark
class.description:
Divine Spark. As a [action]Magic[/action] action, you point your Holy Symbol at another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself and focus divine energy at it. Roll 1d8 and add your Wisdom modifier. You either restore Hit Points to the creature equal to that total or force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice) equal to that total. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage (round down).

You roll an additional d8 when you reach Cleric levels 7 (2d8), 13 (3d8), and 18 (4d8).
class.snippet: You point your Holy Symbol at another creature you can see within 30 ft. of you and focus divine energy at it. You either: • Restore {{1+(classlevel/7)@rounddown,max:1+(classlevel/13)@rounddown+(classlevel/18)@rounddown}}d8{{modifier:wis}} HP to the creature. • Force the creature to make a DC {{savedc:wis}} Con. saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {{1+(classlevel/7)@rounddown,max:1+(classlevel/13)@rounddown+(classlevel/18)@rounddown}}d8{{modifier:wis}} Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice), or half as much on a successful save.

class.name: Channel Divinity: Turn Undead
class.description: Turn Undead. As a [action]Magic[/action] action, you present your Holy Symbol and censure Undead creatures. Each Undead of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its save, it has the [condition]Frightened[/condition] and [condition]Incapacitated[/condition] conditions for 1 minute. For that duration, it tries to move as far from you as it can on its turns. This effect ends early on the creature if it takes any damage, if you have the [condition]Incapacitated[/condition] condition, or if you die.
class.snippet:
You present your Holy Symbol and censure Undead creatures. Each Undead of your choice within 30 ft. of you must make a DC {{savedc:wis}} Wis. saving throw. If the creature fails its save, it has the Frightened and Incapacitated conditions for 1 minute, and for that duration, it tries to move as far from you as it can on its turns.

This effect ends early if the creature takes damage, if you have the Incapacitated condition, or if you die.
class.saveFailDescription: It has the Frightened and Incapacitated conditions for 1 minute, and it tries to move as far from you as it can on its turns.

class.name: Initiate a Circle Spell
class.description: You take a Magic action to initiate casting a Circle spell. When you do so, choose which Circle casting option you’re using for this casting; you must also meet any of the other requirements described in that option’s text. Until the Circle spell’s casting is complete, you must maintain Concentration on the spell.
class.snippet: You take a Magic action to initiate casting a Circle spell. When you do so, choose which Circle casting option you’re using for this casting; you must also meet any of the other requirements described in that option’s text. Until the Circle spell’s casting is complete, you must maintain Concentration on the spell.

class.name: Circle Spell: Augment
class.description: When you cast a spell with a range of at least 5 feet, you can increase the range of the spell by 1,000 feet per secondary caster contributing to the spell, up to a maximum of a 1-mile increase.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell with a range of at least 5 ft., you can increase the range of the spell by 1,000 ft. per secondary caster contributing to the spell, up to a max of a 1-mile increase.

class.name: Circle Spell: Distribute
class.description: When you cast a spell that requires Concentration, you can distribute the mental load of the spell among you and the secondary casters. Once the casting is complete, you and all secondary casters can maintain Concentration on this spell. As long as at least one caster who contributed to the spell maintains this Concentration, the spell’s effects remain active.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell that requires Concentration, you can distribute the mental load of the spell among you and the secondary casters. Once the casting is complete, you and all secondary casters can maintain Concentration on this spell. As long as at least one caster who contributed to the spell maintains this Concentration, the spell’s effects remain active.

class.name: Circle Spell: Expand
class.description:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can increase one dimension of the spell’s area of effect for this casting by 10 feet per secondary caster contributing to the spell. For example, if a spell creates a 20-foot-radius Sphere, casting it as a Circle spell with two secondary casters would increase the radius to 40 feet. A spell that creates a Line that is 5 feet wide and 300 feet long, cast as a Circle spell with two secondary casters, could make a Line that is either 25 feet wide or 320 feet long.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can increase one dimension of the spell’s area of effect for this casting by 10 ft. per secondary caster contributing to the spell. 

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Circle Spell: Prolong
class.description:
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can increase the duration of the spell depending on the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell, as detailed in the table below.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

Spell Duration
Secondary Casters The Spell Increases Duration by
1–3 1 hour
4–6 8 hours
7+ 24 hours
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 min or longer, you can increase the duration of the spell depending on the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Circle Spell: Safeguard
class.description:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can carve out a safe zone within that area of effect that is unaffected by the spell for its duration. This safe zone consists of a number of 5-foot Cubes equal to your spellcasting ability modifier plus the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell (minimum of one Cube). You can arrange the Cubes as you like, but each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube.

If the spell’s area of effect can be moved, the safe zone moves with it.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can carve out a safe zone within that area of effect that is unaffected by the spell for its duration. This safe zone consists of a number of 5-ft. Cubes equal to {{modifier:cha#unsigned}} + the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell (min of 1 Cube). You can arrange the Cubes as you like, but each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube. If the spell’s area of effect can be moved, the safe zone moves with it.

class.name: Circle Spell: Supplant
class.description:
When you cast a spell that requires at least one Material component with a specified cost that is consumed by the spell, you can reduce the minimum cost of one such Material component by 50 GP per each secondary caster contributing to the spell. For example, casting [spells]Revivify[/spells] as a Circle spell with two secondary casters would reduce the Material component’s minimum cost to 200 GP.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot of a level greater than or equal to the spell’s level (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that requires at least one Material component with a specified cost that is consumed by the spell, you can reduce the min cost of one such Material component by 50 GP per each secondary caster contributing to the spell.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot of a level greater than or equal to the spell’s level (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Pull of Death
class.description: Once per turn, when you deal damage to a creature that’s missing any Hit Points by casting a spell or by hitting with an attack roll, that creature takes an extra 1d4 Necrotic damage. This extra damage increases to 1d6 when you reach Cleric level 11.
class.snippet: Once per turn, when you deal damage to a creature that’s missing any HP by casting a spell or by hitting with an attack roll, that creature takes an extra 1d4 Necrotic damage. This extra damage increases to 1d6 when you reach Cleric level 11.

class.name: Channel Divinity: Path to the Grave
class.description: As a Bonus Action, you present your Holy Symbol and expend a use of Channel Divinity to curse one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself until the start of your next turn. While cursed, the creature has [rules]Disadvantage[/rules] on attack rolls and saving throws.
class.snippet: As a Bonus Action, you present your Holy Symbol and expend a use of Channel Divinity to curse one creature you can see within 30 ft. of yourself until the start of your next turn. While cursed, the creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws.

class.name: Path to the Grave: End Curse
class.description: When you or an ally you can see hits the target cursed by Path to the Grave with an attack roll, you can end the curse early (no action required) to make the attack deal extra Necrotic or Radiant damage equal to your Cleric level.
class.snippet: When you or an ally you can see hits the cursed target with an attack roll, you can end the curse early to make the attack deal {{classlevel}} extra Necrotic or Radiant damage.

feat.name: Gathered Whispers: Unearthly Scream
feat.description: When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to channel your haunting spirits into a protective, otherworldly scream. You can add your Proficiency Bonus to your AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
feat.snippet: When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to add your Proficiency Bonus to your AC against that attack. You can use this benefit {{proficiency#unsigned}} times per Long Rest.

feat.name: Gathered Whispers: Voices from Beyond
feat.description: Immediately after you make a [rules]D20 Test[/rules] and roll a 1 on the d20, the haunting voices rise to a ghastly volume. Make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the [condition]Deafened[/condition] condition until the end of your next turn. While Deafened, you have [rules]Disadvantage[/rules] on ability checks and attack rolls.
feat.snippet: Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, make a Wisdom saving throw (DC {{13+proficiency#unsigned}}). On a failed save, you have the Deafened condition until the end of your next turn. While Deafened, you have Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

race.restriction: Made to avoid or end the Charmed condition

definition.name: Guidance
definition.description: You touch a willing creature and choose a skill. Until the spell ends, the creature adds 1d4 to any ability check using the chosen skill.

definition.name: Toll the Dead
definition.description:
You point at one creature you can see within range, and the single chime of a dolorous bell is audible within 10 feet of the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take 1d8 Necrotic damage. If the target is missing any of its Hit Points, it instead takes 1d12 Necrotic damage.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by one die when you reach levels 5 (2d8 or 2d12), 11 (3d8 or 3d12), and 17 (4d8 or 4d12).

modifiers.restriction: The die type becomes a d12 if the target is missing any of its hit points.

definition.name: Sacred Flame
definition.description:
Flame-like radiance descends on a creature that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d8 Radiant damage. The target gains no benefit from Half Cover or Three-Quarters Cover for this save.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

definition.name: Word of Radiance
definition.description:
Burning radiance erupts from you in a 5-foot Emanation. Each creature of your choice that you can see in it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 Radiant damage.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

definition.name: Spiritual Weapon
definition.description:
You create a floating, spectral force that resembles a weapon of your choice and lasts for the duration. The force appears within range in a space of your choice, and you can immediately make one melee spell attack against one creature within 5 feet of the force. On a hit, the target takes Force damage equal to 1d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

As a Bonus Action on your later turns, you can move the force up to 20 feet and repeat the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for every slot level above 2.

definition.name: Prayer of Healing
definition.description:
Up to five creatures of your choice who remain within range for the spell’s entire casting gain the benefits of a Short Rest and also regain 2d8 Hit Points. A creature can’t be affected by this spell again until that creature finishes a Long Rest.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 2.

modifiers.restriction: Affects up to five creatures of your choice.

definition.name: Inflict Wounds
definition.description:
A creature you touch makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d10 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

definition.name: Shield of Faith
definition.description: A shimmering field surrounds a creature of your choice within range, granting it a +2 bonus to AC for the duration.

definition.name: Healing Word
definition.description:
A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 1.

definition.name: Cure Wounds
definition.description:
A creature you touch regains a number of Hit Points equal to 2d8 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

definition.name: Command
definition.description:
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose the command from these options:

Approach. The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

Grovel. The target has the Prone condition and then ends its turn.

Halt. On its turn, the target doesn’t move and takes no action or Bonus Action.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.
